columns.name: normalized_question
columns.system_prompt: You rewrite real GitHub issue / PR threads into concise support questions. Preserve technical fidelity: do not invent facts.

columns.name: support_answer

nodes.name: note_1
nodes.markdown:
### GitHub Support Bot
Real GitHub data to synthetic Q&A pairs for support-bot fine-tuning.

**1. Click the Source Data node (top-left of the canvas) to enter:**
- **Repos**: one `owner/name` per line
- **GitHub token**: your `GH_TOKEN` (or leave blank to use the server's env var)
- Item types (issues / pulls / commits) and per-resource limit

**2. Click `Run` below and set the number of rows** (defaults to 10 for a quick test).

The built-in `github_repo` seed reader does rate-limit-aware GraphQL scraping. Each scraped item becomes a row with `title`, `body`, `comments`, `labels`, etc. Two LLM blocks turn it into `{normalized_question, support_answer}`. Answers are structured JSON with `answer` / `diagnosis_questions` / `cites` / `confidence`.

nodes.name: note_2
nodes.markdown:
The **normalize** step turns a raw GitHub thread (which often has title/body/comments scattered) into a single clean support question.

Tweak the prompt to:
- always include the traceback
- drop off-topic chitchat
- target a specific user persona

nodes.name: note_3
nodes.markdown:
The **answer** block enforces a JSON shape that maps directly onto the grounded RAG answerer format.

Fields:
- `answer` (Markdown with code)
- `diagnosis_questions` (follow-ups)
- `cites` (doc + GitHub URLs)
- `confidence` (high/medium/low)

This is key: uniform schema = less cleanup later.
